Title: Identification of INOSITOL PHOSPHORYLCERAMIDE SYNTHASE 2 (IPCS2) as a new rate-limiting component in Arabidopsis pathogen entry control

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: plasma membrane; focal accumulation at pathogen contact sites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enable polarized secretion of defense cargo to block fungal ingress
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: host-pathogen contact sites / polar plasma membrane domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Focal accumulation intensity of PEN3-GFP at Bgh penetration sites is reduced in the ipcs2W205* background, linking GIPC synthesis to PEN3 delivery
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic crosses; confocal microscopy of pPEN3:PEN3-GFP; time-course and fluorescence quantification; double-mutant pathogenicity assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEN3 is a defense cargo whose polarized delivery to fungal contact sites depends on IPCS2 activity. 2. Frequency and area of PEN3 focal accumulation are largely unchanged, but signal intensity is markedly reduced in ipcs2W205*. 3. Genetic analysis places IPCS2 in the PEN2/PEN3 pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PEN3 is not the primary mutated gene in this study but is a core mechanistic readout directly tested in Results

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Weak constitutive expression in rosette leaves; induced by Bgh infection and further upregulated in ipcs2W205*
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Weak in rosette leaves; pathogen inducible
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor contributor to pathogen entry control; ipcs1 single knockout is wild type-like, while IPCS1 is likely upregulated to partially compensate for IPCS2 loss
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; single-mutant assay; inducible IPCS1;2 amiRNA knockdown assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bgh infection; beta-Estradiol induction in IPCS1;2 amiRNA line
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPCS1 expression increases upon Bgh infection and in the ipcs2W205* background. 2. ipcs1 single mutants do not show enhanced susceptibility, indicating IPCS1 is not the predominant contributor. 3. IPCS1 likely partially compensates when IPCS2 is absent.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly tested in Results as a homolog contributing residual IPC/GIPC synthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: syntaxin of plants SNARE marker of TGN secretory vesicles
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with GIPC-enriched cargo sorting and polar secretion
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: SYP61-secretory vesicles and pathogen-induced focal domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SYP61-CFP accumulates at Bgh appressorial contact sites, supporting SYP61 secretory vesicles as the route for IPCS2/GIPC-dependent defense cargo delivery
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SYP61-labeled secretory vesicles accumulate at fungal contact sites during infection. 2. Together with IPCS2 TGN localization, this supports a model in which GIPCs facilitate cargo sorting into SYP61 vesicles for PEN3 delivery.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a mechanistic trafficking marker rather than mutated focal gene

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: IPCS family sphingolipid synthase; contains conserved catalytic motifs of IPC synthases
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glycosyl inositol phosphorylceramide (GIPC) branch of sphingolipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream to midstream; committed entry into IPC/GIPC synthesis from ceramide
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ceramide; phosphatidylinositol-derived inositol phosphate head group donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an inositol phosphate head group derived from phosphatidylinositol onto ceramide to form IPC, enabling downstream GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor and directly initiates flux toward GIPC production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for pathogen entry control by facilitating polar secretion/sorting of defense cargo PEN3 to fungal penetration sites; also contributes to sphingolipid homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: trans-Golgi network-associated secretory vesicle pathway and polar plasma membrane domains at attempted fungal entry sites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed IPCS isoform in all tissues; transcript induced in leaves upon Bgh infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves; induced by non-adapted powdery mildew infection (Bgh)
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: trans-Golgi network (TGN); co-localizes with SYP61-secretory vesicle subdomain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a new rate-limiting component in Arabidopsis pathogen entry control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of IPCS2 activity increases ceramide levels, reduces IPC and Hex-GIPC levels, impairs PEN3 focal accumulation, and enhances entry success of non-adapted powdery mildew fungi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: forward genetic screen; mutant analysis (ipcs2W205* and ipcs2-1); yeast complementation assay; targeted sphingolipidomics by UPLC-nanoESI-MS/MS; genetic complementation with pUBQ10:IPCS2-mVenus; confocal microscopy; pathogen penetration assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pUBQ10 constitutive promoter; native pPEN3 promoter in marker line; pSYP61 promoter in marker line
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: infection with non-adapted powdery mildews Blumeria graminis f. sp. hordei and Erysiphe pisi; adapted powdery mildew Golovinomyces orontii as comparison
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted sphingolipidomics (UPLC-nanoESI-MS/MS); mapping-by-sequencing with Illumina resequencing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ipcs2W205* shows decreased rosette size and spontaneous lesions at later growth stages; constitutive overexpression of IPCS2-mVenus also causes spontaneous cell death/lesions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering sphingolipid-dependent penetration resistance, but requires tight dosage control because both loss and overexpression can trigger lesion phenotypes and growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPCS2 was identified by forward genetics as a major determinant of Arabidopsis resistance to entry by non-adapted powdery mildews. 2. The ipcs2W205* allele is a functional knockout lacking enzymatic activity in yeast and causing ceramide accumulation with reduced IPC and Hex-GIPC levels in planta. 3. IPCS2 acts at the TGN and is required for efficient polarized accumulation of PEN3 at fungal contact sites. 4. Genetic evidence places IPCS2 in the PEN2/PEN3 pathway and supports IPCS2-derived GIPCs as key components of cell entry control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IPCS2 transcript and IPCS2-mVenus protein levels increase upon pathogen attack; IPCS2 remains at the TGN rather than polarizing to infection sites; both under- and over-accumulation disturb normal physiology; ipcs2-1 shows penetration phenotype despite near wild-type sphingolipid profile, mechanism unresolved

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ceramide hydroxylation supplying IPCS2 substrate pool
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-hydroxylated ceramides for IPCS2 substrate pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the alpha position of the fatty acid moiety in ceramides, contributing to production of IPCS2 substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FAH2, required upstream for full pathogen entry control; fah1 fah2 double mutant shows increased Bgh penetration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH1 acts upstream of IPCS2 by producing hydroxylated ceramide substrates. 2. fah1 fah2 mutants phenocopy enhanced fungal entry, supporting the requirement of IPCS2 products rather than enzyme moonlighting.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied only as part of the fah1 fah2 double mutant in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ceramide hydroxylation supplying IPCS2 substrate pool
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-hydroxylated ceramides for IPCS2 substrate pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the alpha position of the fatty acid moiety in ceramides, contributing to production of IPCS2 substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FAH1, required upstream for full pathogen entry control; fah1 fah2 double mutant shows increased Bgh penetration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH2 acts upstream of IPCS2 by producing hydroxylated ceramide substrates. 2. fah1 fah2 mutants phenocopy enhanced fungal entry, supporting the requirement of IPCS2 products rather than enzyme moonlighting.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied only as part of the fah1 fah2 double mutant in this paper
